articles.title: Rainstorm alert in Satna, Maihar and Umaria today: Bhopal hits 43.7°C, matching 10-year record; Sidhi hotte... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Bhopal sees rain, DJ shed uprooted in Sheopur, 15 hurt:Thunderstorm hits Satna, Maihar and Umaria; alert across half of MP
- Copy link
Even as parts of Madhya Pradesh continued to reel under intense heat, the weather suddenly changed on Thursday. Strong winds, thunder and rain brought relief in districts including Satna, Maihar and Umaria, while conditions in other areas remained hot.
In Bhopal, temperatures reached 43.7°C, equalling a 10-year record and making it the hottest day of the season so far. Meanwhile, in Sheopur, a storm caused chaos when the DJ shed of a hotel was blown away, leaving around 15 people injured.
Khajuraho, which had been among the hottest places in the state, also saw a drop in temperature. The Meteorological Department has indicated that relief from heatwave conditions may continue for the next 3–4 days. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has not issued any heatwave alert for Thursday.
A cyclonic circulation is currently active over the region, and a trough line is also passing through the state, leading to unstable weather conditions.
Districts likely to see rain and thunderstorms today
Rain with thunder and lightning is expected in districts including Gwalior, Sheopur, Morena, Bhind, Datia, Shivpuri, Guna, Ashoknagar, Vidisha, Rajgarh, Agar-Malwa, Neemuch, Mandsaur, Sagar, Niwari, Tikamgarh, Chhatarpur, Panna, Satna, Rewa, Mauganj, Sidhi, Singrauli, Dindori, Mandla, Balaghat, Seoni, Chhindwara and Pandhurna.
Sidhi records 43.8°C, heat grips Raisen and Khajuraho too
Note: Highest temperatures recorded on 29 April, in degrees Celsius
However, districts such as Bhopal, Indore, Ujjain, Jabalpur, Katni, Maihar, Umaria, Shahdol, Anuppur, Narsinghpur, Raisen, Narmadapuram, Betul, Sehore, Harda, Burhanpur, Khandwa, Dewas, Shajapur, Khargone, Barwani, Dhar, Ratlam, Jhabua and Alirajpur are likely to continue experiencing hot weather.
Heatwave conditions and temperature records
On Wednesday, Sidhi recorded the highest temperature in the state at 43.8°C, followed by Raisen at 43.6°C and Narsinghpur-Khandwa at 43°C. Satna recorded 42.9°C, Tikamgarh 42.6°C, and several other districts remained above 42°C.
Among major cities, Bhopal recorded 43.7°C, Indore 40.1°C, Gwalior 39.4°C, Ujjain 40°C, and Jabalpur 40.8°C.
Meteorologists said Bhopal has matched its previous high recorded on 30 April 2019, while the all-time April record remains 44.4°C set in 1996.
Weather system expected to change from May 2
According to the Meteorological Department, a new western disturbance is likely to become active over the western Himalayan region from 2 May, which may bring further weather changes to the state.
Authorities have advised people to avoid stepping out between 12 noon and 3 pm unless necessary, as heat conditions remain most severe during this period.
Heatwave advisory issued
Officials have urged people to stay hydrated, avoid prolonged exposure to sunlight, and wear light cotton clothing. Special care has been advised for children and elderly people.
Earlier weather trend in April
April is traditionally one of the hottest months in Madhya Pradesh, along with May. In recent years, several cities including Bhopal, Indore, Jabalpur and Gwalior have recorded temperatures between 43°C and 45°C, with occasional spells of rain and thunderstorms also seen during the month.
